name: Target Hospitality Corp.
business: Target Hospitality is one of the largest vertically integrated specialty rental and hospitality services companies in North America, providing comprehensive turnkey solutions including specialty rental accommodations, culinary services, and hospitality solutions to customers in natural resource development, critical mineral development, data center infrastructure projects, and the U.S. government.

exposures.sentence: We provide cost-effective and customized specialty rental accommodations, culinary services and hospitality solutions, including site design, construction, operations, security, housekeeping, catering, concierge services and health and recreation facilities.

The WHS segment includes one community in Winnemucca, Nevada to establish a new regional workforce hub network capacity for lithium and related critical mineral development as well as the Workforce Housing Contract for construction of workforce housing and delivery of comprehensive hospitality and facility services.

The WHS segment also includes the Data Center Community Contract described below to construct and provide comprehensive facility services and hospitality solutions supporting the Data Center Community.
Demand for our services is sensitive to the level of exploration, development and production activity of, and the corresponding capital spending by, natural resource development companies.

For the year ended December 31, 2025, approximately 58.5% of our revenue was earned from specialty rental with vertically integrated hospitality, specifically lodging and related ancillary services, whereas the remaining 14.3% of revenues were earned through leasing of lodging facilities and 27.2% of revenues were earned through construction fee income.

catalysts.event: Workforce Housing Contract entered into with Lithium Nevada
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, the Company entered into the Workforce Housing Contract to provide construction of workforce housing, facility services, and hospitality solutions to Lithium Nevada in support of Lithium Nevada's development of Thacker Pass.
Effective February 21, 2025, the PCC Contract was terminated, but Target retained ownership of these assets.
The assets associated with the STFRC Contract were reactivated under the DIPC Contract effective March 5, 2025, which is a lease and services agreement with an anticipated five-year term.

On March 25, 2025, the Company redeemed $181.4 million aggregate principal amount of the 2025 Senior Secured Notes for a redemption price equal to 101.000% of the principal amount of the 2025 Senior Secured Notes plus accrued and unpaid interest.

During the year ended December 31, 2025, the Company entered into the Data Center Community Contract to construct and provide comprehensive facility services and hospitality solutions supporting the Data Center Community.

catalysts.event: First occupancy of Workforce Hub began
catalysts.sentence: first occupancy that began in September 2025.

catalysts.event: First occupancy of Data Center Community began
catalysts.sentence: first occupancy of the Community began in September 2025 for the initial 250 beds.

catalysts.event: Ramp up period for DIPC Contract completed
catalysts.sentence: The ramp up period was completed as scheduled as of September 30, 2025 with the maximum fixed minimum revenue amount now being recognized.

catalysts.event: Expanded Community Contract (Data Center) adding 800 beds
catalysts.sentence: During the three months ended December 31, 2025, the scope of the Data Center Community Contract was amended to add an additional 800 beds to the Data Center Community by June 2026.

In December 2025, the Company announced a 25-month contract award agreement to build and operate a community in Northern Nevada, supporting power generation expansion for mining and data center projects (the Power Community Contract).

catalysts.event: Power Community Contract expected to start generating revenue
catalysts.sentence: It is expected to generate approximately $35 million in revenue over its initial 25-month term starting in June of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: The potential total addressable market opportunity for integrated workforce hospitality solutions supporting data center development, AI infrastructure, critical mineral development, and power generation projects is approximately $18 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Management estimates the potential total addressable market opportunity for integrated workforce hospitality solutions supporting data center development, AI infrastructure, critical mineral development, and power generation projects to be approximately $18 billion.
